mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in payroll employment signals strong labor market conditions, which drive corporate earnings growth and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high employment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% indicates a tightening monetary policy, which raises borrowing costs and reduces corporate investment, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite high rate signals, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous expansion in GDP and housing starts signals robust demand conditions, which boost corporate revenues and stock valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in co-expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply-side constraints, as indicated by declining industrial production, equity markets face inflationary pressures that lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite declining industrial production, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.3% signals rising inflation, which prompts central bank tightening and reduces equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite high inflation signals, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle, which lowers discount rates and boosts equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising payroll employment and falling interest rates indicates a strong labor market and accommodative monetary policy, which together drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in co-optimistic periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion regimes, characterized by high industrial production, equity markets face overvaluation risks that lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite high industrial production, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
